heading: Supporting research and technical analysis

children.heading: Program direction
children.label: 1 Program direction
children.text: The Secretary shall continue to support a vigorous program of basic energy sciences to provide basic research support for the development of energy technologies. Such program shall focus on the efficient production and use of energy, and the expansion of our knowledge of materials, chemistry, geology, and other related areas of advancing technology development.

children.label: A
children.text: As part of the program referred to in paragraph (1), the Secretary shall carry out planning, construction, and operation of user facilities to provide special scientific and research capabilities, including technical expertise and support as appropriate, to serve the research needs of our Nation’s universities, industry, private laboratories, Federal laboratories, and others. Research institutions or individuals from other nations shall be accommodated at such user facilities in cases where reciprocal accommodations are provided to United States research institutions and individuals or where the Secretary considers such accommodation to be in the national interest.

children.label: B
children.text: The construction of the Advanced Photon Source at the Argonne National Laboratory is hereby authorized.

children.label: C
children.text: The Secretary shall not change the user fee practice in effect as of , with respect to user facilities unless the Secretary notifies Congress 90 days before the effective date of any change.October 1, 1991

children.label: D
children.text: The Secretary shall expedite the design for construction of the Advanced Neutron Source at the Oak Ridge National Laboratory, in order to provide critical research capabilities in support of our national research initiatives for advanced materials and biotechnology, as well as a broad range of research. Such action shall be consistent with the Basic Energy Sciences Advisory Committee’s Technical Evaluation of accelerator and reactor neutron source technologies. Within 90 days after , the Secretary shall submit to the Congress a plan for such design, including a schedule for construction.October 24, 1992

children.label: A
children.text: as otherwise provided for in cooperative research and development agreements or other agreements entered into under existing law;

children.label: B
children.text: for fees for user facilities, as determined by the Secretary; or

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of specific projects, where the Secretary determines that the benefits of such research and development accrue to a specific industry or group of industries, in which case cost sharing under shall apply.section 13542 of this title

children.heading: University research reactors
children.label: 1 University research reactors
children.text: The Secretary shall support programs for improvements and upgrading of university research reactors and associated instrumentation and equipment. Within 1 year after , the Secretary shall submit to the Congress a report on the condition and status of university research reactors, which includes a 5-year plan for upgrading and improving such facilities, instrumentation capabilities, and related equipment.October 24, 1992

children.heading: Method to evaluate effectiveness of education programs
children.label: 2 Method to evaluate effectiveness of education programs
children.text: The Secretary shall develop a method to evaluate the effectiveness of science and mathematics education programs provided by the Department of Energy and its laboratories, including specific evaluation criteria.

children.heading: Established program to stimulate competitive research
children.label: 3 Established program to stimulate competitive research

children.heading: Eligible jurisdiction
children.label: i Eligible jurisdiction
children.text: The term “eligible jurisdiction” means a State that is determined to be eligible for a grant under this paragraph in accordance with subparagraph (D).

children.heading: EPSCoR
children.label: ii EPSCoR
children.text: The term “EPSCoR” means the Established Program to Stimulate Competitive Research operated under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: National Laboratory
children.label: iii National Laboratory
children.text: The term “National Laboratory” has the meaning given the term in .section 15801 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: a State;

children.label: II
children.text: the District of Columbia;

children.label: III
children.text: the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico;

children.label: IV
children.text: Guam; and

children.label: V
children.text: the United States Virgin Islands.

children.heading: Program operation
children.label: B Program operation
children.text: The Secretary shall operate an Established Program to Stimulate Competitive Research.

children.label: i
children.text: to increase the number of researchers in eligible jurisdictions, especially at institutions of higher education, capable of performing nationally competitive science and engineering research in support of the mission of the Department of Energy in the areas of applied energy research, environmental management, and basic science;

children.label: ii
children.text: to improve science and engineering research and education programs at institutions of higher education in eligible jurisdictions and enhance the capabilities of eligible jurisdictions to develop, plan, and execute research that is competitive, including through investing in research equipment and instrumentation; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to increase the probability of long-term growth of competitive funding to eligible jurisdictions.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary may establish criteria for determining whether a State is eligible for a grant under this paragraph.

children.label: I
children.text: historically has received relatively little Federal research and development funding; and

children.label: aa
children.text: to develop the research bases in the State; and

children.label: bb
children.text: to improve science and engineering research and education programs at institutions of higher education in the State.

children.heading: Eligibility under NSF EPSCoR
children.label: iii Eligibility under NSF EPSCoR
children.text: At the election of the Secretary, or if the Secretary declines to establish criteria under clause (i), the Secretary may continue to use the eligibility criteria in use on , or any successor criteria.January 1, 2021

children.label: I
children.text: energy efficiency, fossil energy, renewable energy, nuclear energy, and other applied energy research;

children.label: II
children.text: electricity delivery research;

children.label: III
children.text: cybersecurity, energy security, and emergency response;

children.label: IV
children.text: environmental management; and

children.label: aa
children.text: advanced scientific computing research;

children.label: bb
children.text: basic energy sciences;

children.label: cc
children.text: biological and environmental research;

children.label: dd
children.text: fusion energy sciences;

children.label: ee
children.text: high energy physics;

children.label: ff
children.text: nuclear physics;

children.label: gg
children.text: isotope research, development, and production;

children.label: hh
children.text: accelerator research, development, and production; and

children.label: ii
children.text: other areas of research funded by the Office of Science, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: to support research that is carried out in partnership with the National Laboratories;

children.label: II
children.text: to provide for undergraduate scholarships, graduate fellowships, and traineeships;

children.label: III
children.text: to support research by early career faculty and staff;

children.label: IV
children.text: to improve research capabilities through annual research implementation grants;

children.label: V
children.text: to develop research clusters for particular areas of expertise; and

children.label: VI
children.text: to diversify the future workforce.

children.heading: No cost sharing
children.label: iii No cost sharing
children.text: EPSCoR shall not impose any cost-sharing requirement with respect to a grant made under this subparagraph, but may require letters of commitment from National Laboratories.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Pursuant to subparagraph (E)(ii), the Secretary shall award grants to institutions of higher education in eligible jurisdictions for those institutions of higher education to provide scholarships and fellowships.

children.label: AA
children.text: shall be for a period of 1 year; and

children.label: BB
children.text: may be competitively renewable on an annual basis; and

children.label: bb
children.text: in the case of a graduate level fellowship, shall be for a period of not more than 5 years.

children.label: aa
children.text: to support investigator-initiated research, including associated research equipment and instrumentation;

children.label: bb
children.text: to support activities associated with identifying and responding to funding opportunities;

children.label: cc
children.text: to secure technical assistance for the pursuit of funding opportunities; and

children.label: dd
children.text: to develop and enhance collaboration among National Laboratories, Department of Energy programs, the private sector, and other relevant entities.

children.label: aa
children.text: for a period of not more than 5 years; and

children.label: bb
children.text: competitively renewable for an additional 5-year period.

children.label: aa
children.text: developing expertise in key technology areas, including associated equipment and instrumentation;

children.label: bb
children.text: developing and acquiring novel, state-of-the-art instruments and equipment that range in cost from $500,000 to $20,000,000;

children.label: cc
children.text: enhancing collaboration with National Laboratories, the Department of Energy, and the private sector through faculty or staff placement programs; and

children.label: dd
children.text: supporting formal partnership programs with institutions of higher education and National Laboratories.

children.label: aa
children.text: for a period of not more than 5 years; and

children.label: bb
children.text: renewable for an additional 5-year period.

children.heading: Equipment and instrumentation
children.label: III Equipment and instrumentation
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall ensure that research equipment and instrumentation developed or acquired pursuant to a grant awarded under subclause (I) may sustain continued operation and be maintained without the need for additional or subsequent funding under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 270 days after , the Secretary shall submit to the Committees on Energy and Natural Resources and Appropriations of the Senate and the Committees on Energy and Commerce and Appropriations of the House of Representatives a plan describing how the Secretary shall implement EPSCoR.January 1, 2021

children.label: I
children.text: the management structure of EPSCoR, which shall ensure that all research areas and activities described in this paragraph are incorporated into EPSCoR;

children.label: II
children.text: efforts to conduct outreach to inform eligible jurisdictions and faculty of changes to, and opportunities under, EPSCoR;

children.label: III
children.text: how EPSCoR plans to increase engagement with eligible jurisdictions, faculty, and State committees, including by holding regular workshops, to increase participation in EPSCoR; and

children.label: IV
children.text: any other issues relating to EPSCoR that the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: I
children.text: update the plan submitted under clause (i); and

children.label: II
children.text: submit the updated plan to the committees described in that clause.

children.label: I
children.text: the tangible progress made towards achieving the objectives described in subparagraph (C);

children.label: II
children.text: the impact of research supported by EPSCoR on the mission of the Department of Energy; and

children.label: III
children.text: any other issues relating to EPSCoR that the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: The organization with which the Secretary contracts under clause (i) shall not be a National Laboratory.

children.heading: Report
children.label: iii Report
children.text: Not later than 6 years after , the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on Science, Space and Technology and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives a report describing the results of the assessment carried out under clause (i), including recommendations for improvements that would enable the Secretary to achieve the objectives described in subparagraph (C).January 1, 2021

children.label: I
children.text: the total amount of expenditures made by the Department to carry out EPSCoR in each eligible jurisdiction for each of the 3 most recent fiscal years for which such information is available;

children.label: aa
children.text: the number of EPSCoR awards made to institutions of higher education located in eligible jurisdictions; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the amount and type of each award;

children.label: III
children.text: the number of awards that are not EPSCoR awards made by the Secretary to institutions of higher education located in eligible jurisdictions;

children.label: aa
children.text: the number of representatives of institutions of higher education in eligible jurisdictions serving on each Office of Science advisory committee; and

children.label: bb
children.text: for each such advisory committee, the percentage of committee membership that those individuals constitute; and

children.label: V
children.text: the number of individuals from institutions of higher education in eligible jurisdictions serving on peer review committees.

children.label: I
children.text: $50,000,000 for fiscal year 2023;

children.label: II
children.text: $50,000,000 for fiscal year 2024;

children.label: III
children.text: $75,000,000 for fiscal year 2025;

children.label: IV
children.text: $100,000,000 for fiscal year 2026; and

children.label: V
children.text: $100,000,000 for fiscal year 2027.

children.label: I
children.text: the full amount of funds expended to provide the grant towards meeting the funding requirement in clause (iii) if the lead entity of the consortium is an institution of higher education located in an eligible jurisdiction; and

children.label: II
children.text: only the funds provided to institutions of higher education located in eligible jurisdictions towards meeting the funding requirement in clause (iii) if the lead entity of the consortium is an institution of higher education that is not located in an eligible jurisdiction.

children.heading: Additional funds for eligible jurisdictions
children.label: iii Additional funds for eligible jurisdictions
children.text: In addition to funds authorized to be appropriated under clause (i), the Secretary, to the maximum extent practicable while maintaining the competitive, merit-based award processes of the Office of Science, shall ensure that, of the research and development funds of the Office of Science that are awarded by the Secretary each year to institutions of higher education, not less than 10 percent is awarded to institutions of higher education in eligible jurisdictions pursuant to the evaluation and selection criteria in section 605.10 of title 10, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations).

children.heading: Additional funds for equipment and instrumentation
children.label: iv Additional funds for equipment and instrumentation
children.text: In addition to funds authorized to be appropriated under clause (i), there is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to award grants under subparagraph (F)(iii)(I) for the purpose described in item (bb) of that subparagraph $25,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: Accounting
children.label: v Accounting
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall ensure that each program within the Department of Energy that endorses an EPSCoR grant awardee shall contribute funding to the award to acknowledge the research benefits to the mission of that program.

children.heading: Technology transfer
children.label: c Technology transfer
children.text: The Secretary shall support technology transfer activities conducted by the National Laboratories. Within 1 year after , the Secretary shall submit to the Congress a report on the adequacy of funding for such activities, along with a proposal recommending ways to reduce the length of time required to consummate cooperative research and development agreements.October 24, 1992

children.heading: Facilities support for multiprogram energy laboratories
children.label: d Facilities support for multiprogram energy laboratories

children.heading: Facility policy
children.label: 1 Facility policy
children.text: The Secretary shall develop and implement a least cost strategy for correcting facility problems, closing unneeded facilities, making facility modifications, and building new facilities at multiprogram energy laboratories.

children.label: A
children.text: Providing for the safety and health of employees, visitors, and the general public with regard to correcting existing structural, mechanical, electrical, and environmental deficiencies.

children.label: B
children.text: Providing for the repair and rehabilitation of existing facilities to keep them in use and prevent deterioration.

children.label: C
children.text: Providing engineering design and construction services for those facilities which require modification or additions in order to meet the needs of new or expanded programs.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: e Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary for Supporting Research and Technical Analysis, including Basic Energy Sciences, Energy Research Analysis, University and Science Education, Technology Transfer, Advisory and Oversight Program Direction, and Facilities Support for Multiprogram Energy Laboratories, $966,804,000 for fiscal year 1993 and such sums as may be necessary for fiscal year 1994.
